title: Conflicted between Goal-Directed and Habitual Control, an fMRI Investigation.

assets.abstract: "Slips of action" occur in everyday life when we momentarily lose sight of a goal (for example, when in a rush or distracted). Associative models propose that these habitual responses can be activated via a direct stimulus-response (S-R) mechanism, regardless of the current hedonic value of the outcome. The slips-of-action task (SOAT) has been extensively used in both healthy and pathological populations to measure habit tendencies, the likelihood of making erroneous responses for devalued outco

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Mechanicality — habit as automated behavior, loss of conscious control, automaticity.
